acceptance_criteria: Activity from suspended domain returns 403; activity from silenced domain is accepted but post does not appear in public timeline; suspended actor's WebFinger is returned (actor lookup still works) but posts are invisible; domain block applied retroactively to cached posts
description: Check domain_blocks table on every inbound activity before processing. Suspended domains: reject all activities with 403 and do not process. Silenced domains: accept activities but do not surface posts in public/federated timeline (only home timeline for users who explicitly follow those accounts). reject_media flag: accept activities but discard media attachments.
design: Check domain from actor_uri on every POST to /inbox. Extract domain from URI. Look up in domain_blocks. suspend → 200 OK (don't leak that we're blocking) or 403. silence → process but mark remote_posts with a silenced flag or filter at query time. add domain column to remote_posts for efficient filtering.
title: Domain block enforcement on inbound activities
